data.title: Immuneering prices $175 million public offering and $25 million private placement of Class A common stock.
data.description: Biotech company Immuneering has disclosed the pricing of its underwritten public offering of Class A Common Stock, totaling $175 million, and a concurrent private placement of Class A shares amounting to $25 million.

data.title: Rising crypto treasury buybacks reflect a 'credibility race', with companies vying to offer attractive setups - Kadan Stadelmann.
data.description: Kadan Stadelmann, the CTO of Komodo Platform, mentioned to a news outlet that treasury firms are currently striving to develop the most appealing setup, as reported by Cointelegraph.

data.title: Liliana "Lili" Gil receives Champion of Change Award at the UN for advocacy promoting Latina pay equity and economic inclusion.
data.description: Liliana "Lili" Gil, CEO and Co-Founder of Culture+ Group, has been acknowledged for her ten years of dedication to promoting Latina pay equity and economic inclusion as catalysts for prosperity and development. The recognition took place in New York on September 24, 2025. Gil, who is also a business television commentator and expert in Cultural Intelligence®, has been instrumental in driving these crucial issues forward.

data.title: Intermap Technologies has filed a prospectus supplement, accessible through SEDAR. No specific details provided.
data.description: The supplement to the prospectus, along with the related base shelf prospectus and any document updates, can be found on SEDAR. This provides investors with essential information and ensures transparency in the process. Access to these documents on SEDAR is crucial for stakeholders, with all details easily available and up-to-date. This platform facilitates access to key financial information and regulatory filings, aiding in informed decision-making. It is imperative for interested parties to refer to these documents on SEDAR for accurate data and regulatory compliance.

data.title: 2025 World Coastal Forum in Yancheng, China aims to promote green growth and sustainable measures.
data.description: In Beijing on September 24, 2025, the 2025 World Coastal Forum commenced in Yancheng, Jiangsu province. The two-day event, as reported by China Daily, focuses on promoting collaboration, building together, and exchanging ideas on ecological conservation and sustainable practices.

data.title: Philippines adopts blockchain to monitor public works spending post mass protests against corruption.
data.description: The government of the Philippines has introduced a new blockchain system aimed at monitoring the expenditures on public works projects. This initiative comes in response to recent demonstrations highlighting issues of corruption within the country. The system will provide increased transparency and accountability in how public funds, totaling a significant amount, are utilized for infrastructure development.

data.title: Trump administration blocking entry of Giant brand bikes made in Taiwan due to forced labor issues - seizing at US border.
data.description: The U.S. government announced plans to confiscate Giant brand bicycles produced in Taiwan at the border due to evidence of forced labor. The Trump administration took action following an investigation that revealed the use of exploitative labor practices in the manufacturing process. The move marks a significant crackdown on human rights abuses in the supply chain of Giant bicycles, impacting imports of the popular Taiwanese brand into the U.S. The decision underscores the importance of ethical sourcing and labor practices in the global market.

data.title: Probe sought into Trump officials David Sacks and Steve Witkoff over alleged UAE crypto gains. Elizabeth Warren calls for investigation.
data.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren and Sen. Elissa Slotkin have called for an investigation into potential conflicts of interest involving Trump administration officials David Sacks and Steve Witkoff for their involvement with the World Liberty Financial cryptocurrency venture. The senators are concerned about their roles in advocating for the U.S. to sell sensitive national security technology to the United Arab Emirates. The lawmakers sent a letter requesting a probe to the acting inspector general of the Commerce Department, the acting director of the Office of Government Ethics, and the acting inspector general at the State Department. Reports suggest that Sacks, Trump's AI and Crypto Czar, and Witkoff, U.S. Special Envoy to the Middle East, played key roles in relaxing national security restrictions on the shipment of advanced AI systems to the UAE. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Exchange balances of Ethereum dropped to a 9-year low of 14.8M ETH as digital asset firms buy in, amid a 'Wall Street glow up'.
data.description: The balances of Ethereum held on exchanges have hit a record low of 14.8 million ETH, the lowest in nine years. This drop comes as digital asset treasury firms and exchange-traded funds ramp up their purchases of the cryptocurrency.
